content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer array of 4 elements `{1, 2, 3, 4}`.

Your task is to demonstrate the use of **pointers with arrays** in C.

Write a program that uses a pointer to access and print each element of the array on a single line separated by a space.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. No further input is required for each test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the four integers 1 2 3 4 separated by a single space on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: T=1, so the program prints the fixed array once.
T=2, the array is printed on two separate lines.
T=3, the array is printed three times, each on its own line.

solution.approach: The array is fixed: int arr[4] = {1,2,3,4}. For each test case we initialise a pointer to the first element (int *ptr = arr) and iterate four times, printing the value pointed to and advancing the pointer. This demonstrates basic pointer arithmetic while keeping the algorithm O(1) per test case. The total time is O(T) and only O(1) extra space is used.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used.
